candidates.name: Bill Lee
candidates.notes: CEO of Lee Company (family HVAC/plumbing business, $250M+ revenue) before becoming governor — entered politics with no prior elected office. Created Governor's Faith-Based and Community Initiative (2019) — state-funded, partners with all faith traditions. Grace Chapel (Franklin, TN) member — non-denominational megachurch. First wife Carol died in horse-riding accident in 2000. Signed near-total abortion trigger ban, anti-drag Adult Entertainment Act, bathroom bill, constitutional carry, school choice vouchers, anti-CRT legislation. After Covenant School shooting (Nashville, 2023), initially expressed openness to red flag laws but walked it back under Republican pressure. Critics accuse him of building a theocracy. Highest scorer in this dataset at 48/70.

profile.background: Businessman who led family construction and HVAC company (Lee Company); active in faith-based ministries; became 50th governor in 2019 with no prior political experience.

candidates.name: Randy McNally
candidates.notes: President of the Tennessee Senate — verified via https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/President_of_the_Tennessee_Senate.

candidates.name: Marsha Blackburn
candidates.notes: Heritage Action lifetime 87% — one of most conservative senators. NRA A rating. Heritage scores: 96% (117th), 85% (115th House). Presbyterian. First woman elected to represent Tennessee in the U.S. Senate. Served 16 years in the House before winning Senate seat. Strong Trump ally. Home Economics degree from Mississippi State — one of few non-lawyer senators. Reelected 2024. Consistently among top Heritage Action scorers. Strong on education (parental rights, anti-CRT, anti-gender ideology) and self-defense (perfect 10). Scores 0 on PCH/CBG — mainstream conservative rather than explicit Christian nationalist.

profile.education: B.S. Home Economics, Mississippi State University (1973)
profile.background: Businesswoman and former Tennessee State Senator. Served in U.S. House from 2003-2019. First woman elected to U.S. Senate from Tennessee. Marketing and management background.
profile.office: 357 Dirksen Senate Office Building

district_offices.address: 10 West M. L. King Blvd, 6th Floor

candidates.name: Bill Hagerty
candidates.notes: Heritage Action lifetime ~85%. Former US Ambassador to Japan (2017-2019, Trump appointee, confirmed 86-12). Private equity background — Boston Consulting Group, then founded Hagerty Peterson & Company. Vanderbilt Law grad. Episcopalian — St. George's Episcopal Church, Nashville. Self-describes as 'Husband | Father of four | Businessman | Community Volunteer | Conservative Republican.' Strong on trade policy and China hawks. One of wealthiest senators (~$50M net worth). Business conservative rather than cultural warrior — scores 0 on PCH/CBG. Perfect 10 on self-defense. First term senator, next election 2026.

profile.education: B.A. Economics, Vanderbilt University (1981); J.D. Vanderbilt Law School (1984)
profile.background: Former U.S. Ambassador to Japan. Private equity executive and Boston Consulting Group alum. Tennessee Economic Development Commissioner. White House Fellow under George H.W. Bush.
profile.office: 251 Russell Senate Office Building
